name: Chocolate Hazelnut Torte
recipeIngredient: 100 grams chocolate, chopped into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Chop the chocolate into small pea-sized pieces. Do not reduce it to a floury consistency. The pieces should remain visible in the cake after baking.
2. Cream butter with sugar until light and fluffy.
3. Add eggs, one at a time, mixing well after each addition.
4. Stir in vanilla extract.
5. Gradually add flour, hazelnuts, baking powder, rum, cinnamon, and cacao powder to the butter mixture. If using a machine, mix all dry ingredients together first before adding them slowly. Reserve some sugar to mix with cacao if hand-mixing to minimize lumps.
6. Gently fold in the chocolate pieces.
7. Carefully fold in the chopped cherries (reserve about 300 g; use more or less as needed).
8. Transfer the batter into a well-buttered 26 cm (10 in) springform pan with an insert that creates a hole in the center for easy removal. Dust the pan lightly if desired.
9. Bake at 350°F (175°C) until golden brown and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, about 50-65 minutes. The cake's moisture content means it can be overbaked easily; test multiple times to ensure doneness.
10. Let cool in the pan for 10 minutes before turning it out onto a wire rack. Decorate with powdered sugar or chocolate glaze, and/or whole nuts if desired.
Note: Sour cherries provide a more intense flavor than sweet ones, but any available cherries will work. The moisture from the cherries contributes to the cake's texture.
